changes_summary.overview: This document summarizes the updates made to the vuln-remediation-concert mode to support the new bob-shell-v2.yml workflow while maintaining backward compatibility with Bob IDE interactive usage.

context.name: bob_shell
context.description: Automated execution via GitHub Actions workflow

characteristics.item: Workflow provides PR URL directly in prompt
Workflow specifies exact branch name to commit to
Must use GitHub MCP correctly with full URLs

changes.change: Added context detection for bob-shell vs bob-ide execution
Added instructions to extract PR URL from workflow prompt
Added instructions to extract branch name from prompt or metadata
Added critical note about using fetch_github_issue with issue_url parameter
Added github_mcp_usage_contexts section explaining two contexts
Added critical instructions for using issue_url parameter
Enhanced PR URL handling with complete examples
Added bob_shell_workflow section to critical_principle

key_sections.section: user_provides_pr_url scenario - Enhanced with full URL handling

changes.change: Added execution_context_detection section
Added bob_shell_execution and bob_ide_execution subsections
Enhanced Phase 1 with context-specific PR fetching
Enhanced Phase 5 with priority-based branch identification

key_sections.section: execution_context_detection - Detects and adapts to context

changes.change: Added execution contexts to overview
Added bob_shell_emphasis to critical_principle
Added priority_order for branch identification
Added from_workflow_prompt section with extraction examples
Enhanced checkout_pr_branch step with context-specific instructions

file.name: 8_github_mcp_for_bob_shell.xml
file.purpose: Dedicated instructions for correct GitHub MCP usage in bob-shell context, addressing common failures and providing clear examples.

changes.change: Explains why GitHub MCP fails in bob-shell
Provides correct usage patterns with examples
Shows incorrect patterns to avoid
Includes troubleshooting guide
Provides quick reference for common operations

improvement.title: Context-Aware Execution
improvement.description: Mode now detects whether it's running in bob-shell (automated) or bob-ide (interactive) context and adapts its behavior accordingly.

improvement.title: Correct GitHub MCP Usage
improvement.description: Clear instructions on parsing PR URLs and using pull_request_read with method, owner, repo, and pullNumber parameters. Also using pull_request_review_write for adding PR comments. This prevents common MCP failures.

improvement.title: Priority-Based Branch Identification
improvement.description: Branch name is now identified with clear priority: workflow prompt first, PR metadata second. This ensures correct branch usage in all contexts.

improvement.title: Comprehensive Examples
improvement.description: Added complete working examples for both bob-shell and bob-ide contexts, including exact tool usage patterns and expected outputs.

improvement.title: Troubleshooting Guide
improvement.description: New file (8_github_mcp_for_bob_shell.xml) provides dedicated troubleshooting for common GitHub MCP issues in automated context.

critical_change.title: ALWAYS Parse PR URLs and Use Correct MCP Tools
critical_change.description: When the workflow prompt contains a PR URL, you MUST: 1. Parse the URL to extract owner, repo, and pullNumber 2. Use pull_request_read with method="get" parameter 3. Provide all components separately (no issue_url parameter exists)
critical_change.example:
Step 1: Parse "https://github.com/org/repo/pull/123" - owner = "org" - repo = "repo" - pullNumber = 123
Step 2: Use pull_request_read <use_mcp_tool> <server_name>github</server_name> <tool_name>pull_request_read</tool_name> <arguments> { "method": "get", "owner": "org", "repo": "repo", "pullNumber": 123 } </arguments> </use_mcp_tool>

critical_change.title: Branch Name Priority
critical_change.description:
Priority 1: Use branch name from workflow prompt if explicitly provided Priority 2: Extract from PR metadata (head.ref) if not in prompt
Never create a new branch - always use the existing PR branch.

critical_change.title: Never Create New PR
critical_change.description:
The workflow has already created the PR. Your job is to: 1. Fetch the existing PR 2. Fix the code 3. Commit to the existing PR branch 4. Add a comment documenting fixes
Never use create_pull_request or similar tools.

backward_compatibility.title: Maintaining Bob IDE Compatibility
backward_compatibility.approach: All changes maintain backward compatibility with Bob IDE interactive usage. The mode detects the execution context and adapts its behavior while following the same core workflow.

bob_ide_unchanged.item: Can still ask "What are the GitHub PRs assigned to me?"
Can still provide PR number or URL interactively
All existing workflows continue to function

steps.step: Trigger bob-shell-v2.yml workflow with a failing PR
Verify mode correctly extracts PR URL from prompt
Verify mode uses fetch_github_issue with issue_url
Verify mode identifies correct branch from prompt
Verify mode commits to existing PR branch
Verify mode adds comment to PR
Ask "What are the GitHub PRs assigned to me?"
Verify mode extracts branch from PR metadata
Verify all interactive features still work

workflow_step.name: run-bob-shell-vuln-remediation
workflow_step.description: The workflow job that triggers this mode with specific prompt format
workflow_step.prompt_format:
Fix the failing unit tests in PR #[number].
Follow this workflow: 1. Analyze the PR at https://github.com/[org]/[repo]/pull/[number] 2. Understand what package was upgraded and why tests are failing 3. Run the unit tests locally to see the exact failures 4. Fix the code to work with the upgraded package 5. Verify all tests pass 6. Commit your fixes to the EXISTING PR branch: [branch-name] 7. Push the changes 8. Add a comment to the PR documenting what was fixed
CRITICAL: Commit to branch '[branch-name]' - do NOT create a new branch!

mode_response.step: Parses URL to get owner, repo, pullNumber

what_changed.item: Added context detection for bob-shell vs bob-ide

why_changed.item: Fix GitHub MCP failures in automated context
Ensure correct branch usage from workflow
Provide clear guidance for both contexts

impact.item: Mode now works reliably in GitHub Actions
GitHub MCP usage is correct and consistent
Troubleshooting is easier with dedicated guide
